topic: Sharp inflation frontier for recompute-based conservative variance estimation under local interference.

open_problems.open_problem: On graph families with genuinely overlapping local exposure neighborhoods, is the fractional-chromatic effective exposure count n/chi_f(D), or a propensity-weighted analogue, a minimax lower bound for uniformly valid design-based confidence length rather than an artifact of disjoint clique-block compression?

source_refs.excerpt: Test whether the lower-bound construction extends from disjoint clique blocks to a broader nondegenerate family

open_problems.why_unsolved: The banked converse uses balanced disjoint clique blocks, where exposure information compresses to independent rare block observations. That construction may reproduce an ordinary rare-treatment lower bound without isolating a genuinely overlapping-neighborhood obstruction. Extending it requires fuzzy schedule priors or an Assouad construction whose induced assignment-outcome laws remain close after informative overlapping exposures are observed, while retaining a computable graph-dependence parameter. This is directly relevant to an all-update-law converse because disconnected and multiscale update sets create the same gap between a convenient block witness and a genuinely geometric obstruction.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A graph-family minimax confidence-length lower-bound theorem, proved by a fuzzy Le Cam or Assouad construction and matched by a chi_f-adaptive interval upper bound in an explicit overlap and propensity regime.

writing_exemplar.why: Its progression from finite-sample randomization inference to a matched effective-sample-size lower bound is the closest framing for the reviewer-prescribed extension.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: randomization permits finite-sample treatment-effect intervals -> dependence reduces effective information -> block examples yield matching width bounds -> what is unavoidable under genuinely overlapping network exposures?

method_exemplars.technique: Build least-favorable randomized-experiment subfamilies and prove nonasymptotic confidence-length lower bounds by information-distance testing arguments.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The requested resolution needs the same upper-versus-converse architecture, with overlapping exposure laws replacing independent treatment observations.

method_exemplars.technique: Apply the b-fold-color Holder inequality to moment-generating functions to obtain concentration controlled by fractional chromatic number.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It supplies the matching upper-bound dependence parameter whose necessity the graph-family lower bound must establish.

open_problems.open_problem: For nonadditive ego-cluster Horvitz-Thompson exposure contrasts, what is the minimal valid conservative variance estimator over the admissible observable quadratic-form class, and when does the completion envelope attain that minimum?

source_refs.excerpt: Vbar_n is a completion supremum, not minimal over quadratic-form variance estimators

open_problems.why_unsolved: The banked Vbar_n is sharp only by definition within its bounded-completion class: it maximizes the exact variance over potential outcomes consistent with observed outcomes. That does not compare Vbar_n with other observable conservative quadratic rules, because unobserved cross-exposure products create a separate admissibility and domination problem. The prior run therefore supplied neither an estimator-class optimality theorem nor a quantitative bound on unavoidable conservativeness, leaving open how completion envelopes compare with recompute-based rules.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A characterization theorem for the admissible observable quadratic bounds, plus an optimizer or sharp lower bound proving whether Vbar_n is minimal and quantifying its unavoidable excess over the oracle variance.

writing_exemplar.why: Its motivation begins with nonestimable design variance and turns that failure into optimization over conservative estimable bounds.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: interference makes exact randomization variance nonestimable -> many conservative quadratic bounds remain valid -> admissibility removes dominated choices -> which bound minimizes conservativeness for ego-cluster HT?

method_exemplars.technique: Translate variance-bound validity into matrix inequalities and optimize over the resulting convex class, using Loewner domination to certify admissibility.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The requested resolution is an estimator-class comparison among conservative quadratic forms specialized to ego-cluster covariance geometry.

method_exemplars.technique: Construct observable conservative variance estimates by deleting assignment coordinates and recomputing the estimator, then separate oracle and proxy contributions.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Recomputation supplies a structurally different admissible subclass against which completion-envelope minimality and excess inflation can be tested.

open_problems.open_problem: What is the sharp minimax excess-inflation rate over all outcome-blind update-set laws and all observable recompute proxies in the Neyman-Jackknife class for an n-cycle with radius-M interference?

source_refs.claim: The paper gives a general conservative recomputation framework and numerical evidence on tightness, but does not characterize an optimal inflation rate over its full class of update laws and observable proxies.
source_refs.author: Bryan Park and Stefan Wager

source_refs.claim: The paper characterizes admissible estimable quadratic variance bounds but supplies no magnitude-of-conservativeness rate indexed by an interference radius.
source_refs.author: Christopher Harshaw, Joel A. Middleton, and Fredrik Savje
source_refs.venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association

open_problems.why_unsolved: Park and Wager expose an oracle-tightness versus proxy-error tradeoff and study random blocks, while Harshaw, Middleton, and Savje provide a qualitative admissibility frontier for quadratic-form bounds. Neither framework supplies a converse uniform over disconnected, multiscale, or otherwise arbitrary index-sampling laws and arbitrary measurable proxies. Such a converse must couple a cycle-isoperimetric or Fourier lower bound for the oracle contribution to an information lower bound caused by outcomes deleted from every admissible proxy; optimizing the displayed block upper bound alone cannot establish minimaxity.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A matched upper-and-lower theorem giving R*(M,n)=1+Theta(sqrt(M/n)) uniformly over a stated M-regime, with an attaining block rule and an all-rule converse over every admissible update law and observable proxy.

writing_exemplar.why: It introduces the exact recompute class and frames tightness as the tradeoff between an oracle Poincare bound and approximation by an observable proxy.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: design-based variance is not point estimable under interference -> recomputation yields conservative bounds without an exposure model -> update size trades oracle tightness against proxy error -> what is the sharp worst-case price of that tradeoff?

method_exemplars.technique: Represent unobservable design-based variance terms as positive-semidefinite slack and characterize the undominated boundary by convex optimization.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It is the closest formal characterization of minimal conservativeness under interference and separates admissibility from a magnitude-sharp frontier.

method_exemplars.technique: Construct nuisance-rich indistinguishable alternatives and dualize Le Cam's two-point modulus into a minimax risk lower bound.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Deleted outcomes form a missing-coordinate observation channel, so the proxy-error half is a functional-estimation lower bound under indistinguishability.

method_exemplars.technique: Analyze moving-block deletion and its variance behavior for dependent observations through block-jackknife asymptotics.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Contiguous update sets on the cycle are the design-based analogue of moving blocks, and the proof tracks how block length enters variance inflation.

open_problems.open_problem: Under approximate rather than exact neighborhood interference, which bandwidth or update-set scale minimizes worst-case conservative-variance inflation while controlling truncation error?

source_refs.claim: After proving conservativeness of an adjusted network-HAC covariance estimator, the paper explicitly leaves open whether its bandwidth satisfies the needed condition and whether alternative bandwidths can do so.
source_refs.author: Mengsi Gao and Peng Ding

source_refs.claim: The paper proposes average-path-length bandwidth selection under approximate neighborhood interference and proves conservativeness under decay and network-growth conditions, not a minimax tuning theorem.
source_refs.author: Michael P. Leung

source_refs.claim: The recompute framework treats decaying dependence as a robustness application but does not give a rate-optimal adaptive deletion scale under an unknown decay envelope.
source_refs.author: Bryan Park and Stefan Wager

open_problems.why_unsolved: With approximate interference, increasing the bandwidth removes dependence bias but enlarges the neighborhood deleted from the proxy and hence its conservativeness and sampling noise. Gao and Ding explicitly leave bandwidth validity open, while Leung's average-path-length rule adapts to network growth but is not shown to minimize a worst-case inflation-plus-truncation criterion. The concrete missing link is a uniform relation among the interference-decay envelope, graph volume growth, and observable-proxy deletion loss.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: An oracle inequality and matching minimax lower bound for a data-independent or adaptive bandwidth rule, explicitly balancing tail-interference truncation against recompute or HAC inflation over a named decay class.

writing_exemplar.why: It states the bandwidth question explicitly after developing a positive-semidefinite conservative network covariance estimator.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: network dependence can make conventional covariance estimates anti-conservative -> a positive adjustment restores conservativeness -> validity depends on bandwidth and ANI conditions -> which bandwidth is valid and rate-optimal?

method_exemplars.technique: Control network-HAC truncation using approximate-neighborhood-interference coefficients and choose scale through network volume growth.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It supplies the exact bias-versus-bandwidth decomposition that a minimax tuning theorem must sharpen.

method_exemplars.technique: Prove network laws of large numbers, central limit theorems, and HAC consistency from explicit tradeoffs between dependence decay and network denseness.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Those tradeoff conditions provide the limit-theory machinery needed to turn a bandwidth oracle inequality into valid inference.

open_problems.open_problem: When does expectation-level conservativeness of a Neyman-Jackknife variance estimator yield uniform design-based Wald coverage under growing local interference?

source_refs.claim: The central guarantee is conservativeness in expectation; the paper does not provide a uniform studentized limit theorem for a growing interference radius and tuned update size.
source_refs.author: Bryan Park and Stefan Wager

source_refs.claim: The paper proves interference central limit theorems by Stein's method and proposes a conservative variance estimator, but does not analyze recompute proxies or their tuning-induced randomness.
source_refs.author: Alex Chin

source_refs.claim: The paper proves asymptotic normality and pointwise-in-bandwidth conservativeness of HAC variance estimators for design-based time-series experiments, not uniform coverage for optimally tuned recompute estimators.
source_refs.author: Zhexiao Lin and Peng Ding

open_problems.why_unsolved: A variance estimator may be conservative in expectation yet have a sufficiently variable lower tail to under-cover after studentization. Local interference makes both the IPW estimator and its recompute variance estimate dependent nonlinear functions of the same assignment, while the candidate optimal block length grows with M and n. Existing dependency-graph central limit theorems and pointwise HAC conservativeness do not jointly control the variance estimator's lower tail uniformly over the finite-population outcome class.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A uniform studentized central limit theorem or finite-sample coverage bound that combines normal-approximation error for the IPW estimator with a one-sided concentration bound for the tuned recompute variance estimator over an explicit M-versus-n regime.

writing_exemplar.why: It motivates exposure-agnostic conservative variance estimation, after which the natural inferential pivot is whether expectation conservativeness delivers coverage.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: interference obstructs unbiased variance estimation -> recomputation guarantees an upper bound in expectation -> reported standard errors share the assignment with the estimator -> when are the resulting confidence intervals uniformly valid?

method_exemplars.technique: Use dependency graphs and Stein's method to bound normal approximation for randomized estimators under interference.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The n-cycle with radius M has an explicit bounded-degree dependency graph, matching the structure required for a quantitative numerator central limit theorem.

method_exemplars.technique: Construct a positive-semidefinite covariance adjustment and prove its asymptotic conservativeness jointly with design-based asymptotic normality.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It provides the nearest studentization proof under network interference, although its pointwise bandwidth argument must be made uniform for recomputation.

method_exemplars.technique: Estimate a sharp variance upper bound consistently and convert it into asymptotically narrowest conservative Wald intervals.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It shows how sharpness of a design-based variance bound becomes coverage and interval-length optimality once bound estimation is controlled.

literature_map:
1. Park-Wager (2026, arXiv:2604.24017) introduces the Neyman Jackknife: conservative design-based variance estimation by recomputing after omitting assignments.
2. Its Bernoulli-cycle analysis exposes an oracle-tightness versus observable-proxy tradeoff and studies uniform random blocks, but gives no minimax rate over all update laws and proxies.
3. Harshaw-Middleton-Savje (2026 JASA; arXiv:2112.01709) characterizes admissible quadratic-form variance bounds and makes their selection a convex program.
4. That paper leaves direct minimization of variance-estimator MSE open; its current MSE criterion uses a potentially loose precision bound.
5. Gao-Ding (2025 Journal of Econometrics; arXiv:2309.07476) constructs positive-semidefinite adjusted network-HAC covariance estimators and explicitly leaves bandwidth validity and alternatives open.
6. Lin-Ding (2025, arXiv:2510.22864) proves design-based asymptotic normality and asymptotic HAC conservativeness for time-series experiments, pointwise rather than minimax in bandwidth.
7. Leung (2022 Econometrica; arXiv:1911.07085) develops approximate neighborhood interference, network-HAC inference, and an average-path-length bandwidth responsive to graph growth.
8. Kojevnikov-Marmer-Song (2021 Journal of Econometrics; arXiv:1903.01059) supplies network limit theory and HAC consistency through dependence-decay and denseness tradeoffs.
9. Chin (2019 Journal of Causal Inference; arXiv:1804.03105) supplies dependency-graph and Stein central limit theorems plus conservative variance analysis under interference.
10. Aronow-Samii (2017 Annals of Applied Statistics; DOI:10.1214/16-AOAS1005) anchors exposure-mapping identification and conservative variance estimation with zero joint-exposure probabilities.
11. Savje-Aronow-Hudgens (2021 Annals of Statistics; arXiv:1711.06399) proves consistency under limited unknown interference but warns that conventional confidence statements can fail.
12. Savje (2024 Biometrika; arXiv:2103.06471) separates exposure-defined estimands from correct interference specification, motivating robustness comparisons for exposure-free recompute bounds.
13. Aronow-Green-Lee (2014 Annals of Statistics; arXiv:1405.6555) shows how sharp variance upper bounds yield asymptotically narrowest conservative Wald intervals under SUTVA.
14. Kunsch (1989 Annals of Statistics; DOI:10.1214/aos/1176347265) is the classical dependent-data block-jackknife technique anchor; Polyanskiy-Wu (2026 Annals of Statistics; arXiv:1902.05616) is the closest modern Le Cam minimax-lower-bound anchor.
prior_proposal_map:
1. Mined accepted exp_bipartite_minimax_design/v1, especially reviews/angle0_v1-v3 and its bank README.
2. Its final reviewer prescribed proving sharp or constant-factor sharpness of the graph-only bounded-outcome variance envelope; this was retained in the map but filtered from open_problems as too remote from local-interference recomputation.
3. Mined downgraded exp_ego_cluster_exposure_clt/ego_cluster_polynomial_clt, including its README and reviews/angle0_v1-v3.
4. That package identifies estimator-class optimality, rather than completion-by-definition sharpness, as its principal unresolved conservative-variance gap.
5. Mined downgraded exp_interference_nonasymp_effcount/holder_fractional_coloring_bernstein_eprocess and reviews/angle0_v1-v3.
6. Its final reviewer asks for a lower-bound extension from disjoint clique blocks to genuinely overlapping-neighborhood graph families.
7. Inspected accepted exp_design_pm1_exactness_boundary_v1/experimentation as an adjacent one-sided-certificate frontier but found its covariance-implementability object less relevant here.
8. No prior rejected-angle file or reviewer suggestion states the exact Park-Wager all-(mu,g) recompute-class sqrt(M/n) converse, so no cross-track entry was merged as source both.
